question: There are different opinions as to what it takes to be a good citizen. As far as you are concerned personally on a scale of 1 to 7, where 1 is not at all important and 7 is very important, how important is it: Always to vote in elections

There are different opinions as to what it takes to be a good citizen. As far as you are concerned personally on a scale of 1 to 7, where 1 is not at all important and 7 is very important, how important is it: Never to try to evade taxes

There are different opinions as to what it takes to be a good citizen. As far as you are concerned personally on a scale of 1 to 7, where 1 is not at all important and 7 is very important, how important is it: Always to obey laws and regulations

There are different opinions as to what it takes to be a good citizen. As far as you are concerned personally on a scale of 1 to 7, where 1 is not at all important and 7 is very important, how important is it: To keep watch on the actions of government

There are different opinions as to what it takes to be a good citizen. There are different opinions about people's rights in a democracy. On a scale of 1 to 7, where 1 is not at all important and 7 is very important, how important is it: That all citizens have an adequate standard of living

There are different opinions about people's rights in a democracy. On a scale of 1 to 7, where 1 is not at all important and 7 is very important, how important is it: That government authorities respect and protect the rights of minorities

There are different opinions about people's rights in a democracy. On a scale of 1 to 7, where 1 is not at all important and 7 is very important, how important is it: That people be given more opportunities to participate in public decision-making

There are different opinions about people's rights in a democracy. On a scale of 1 to 7, where 1 is not at all important and 7 is very important, how important is it: That citizens may engage in acts of civil disobedience when they oppose government actions.

There are different opinions about people's rights in a democracy. On a scale of 1 to 7, where 1 is not at all important and 7 is very important, how important is it: That governments respect democratic rights whatever the circumstances

There are different opinions about people's rights in a democracy. On a scale of 1 to 7, where 1 is not at all important and 7 is very important, how important is it: That people convicted of serious crimes lose their citizen rights

There are different opinions about people's rights in a democracy. On a scale of 1 to 7, where 1 is not at all important and 7 is very important, how important is it: That long-term residents of a country, who are not citizens, have the right to vote in that country’s national elections

There are different opinions about people's rights in a democracy. On a scale of 1 to 7, where 1 is not at all important and 7 is very important, how important is it: That citizens have the right not to vote

There are different opinions about people's rights in a democracy. On a scale of 1 to 7, where 1 is not at all important and 7 is very important, how important is it: That health care be provided for everyone
To what extent do you agree or disagree with the following statements: People like me don’t have any say about what the government does
To what extent do you agree or disagree with the following statements: I don’t think the government cares much what people like me think
To what extent do you agree or disagree with the following statements: I feel I have a pretty good understanding of the important political issues facing (COUNTRY).
To what extent do you agree or disagree with the following statements: I think most people in (COUNTRY) are better informed about politics and government than I am.

Suppose a law were being considered by [appropriate national legislature] that you considered to be unjust or harmful. If such a case arose, how likely is it that you, acting alone or together with others, would be able to try to do something about it?

Suppose a law were being considered by [appropriate national legislature] that you considered to be unjust or harmful. If you made such an effort, how likely is it that [appropriate national legislature] would give serious attention to your demands?
How interested would you say you personally are in politics?
In politics people sometimes talk of left and right. Where would you place yourself on a scale from 0 to 10 where 0 means the left and 10 means the right?
To what extent do you agree or disagree with the following statements: Most of the time we can trust people in government to do what is right
To what extent do you agree or disagree with the following statements: Most politicians are in politics only for what they can get out of it personally

To what extent do you agree or disagree with the following statements: How often do you think that people would try to take advantage of you if they got the chance, and how often would they try to be fair?
option_text: Try to take advantage almost all of the time
Try to take advantage most of the time
Try to be fair most of the time
Try to be fair almost all of the time

question: To what extent do you agree or disagree with the following statements: Generally speaking, would you say that people can be trusted or that you can’t be too careful in dealing with people?
option_text: People can almost always be trusted
You usually can’t be too careful in dealing with people
You almost always can’t be too careful in dealing with people

question: When you get together with your friends, relatives or fellow workers, how often do you discuss politics?
When you hold a strong opinion about politics, how often do you try to persuade your friends, relatives or fellow workers to share your views?
Thinking now about politics in (COUNTRY), to what extent do you agree or disagree with the following statements: Political parties encourage people to become active in politics
Thinking now about politics in (COUNTRY), to what extent do you agree or disagree with the following statements: Political parties do not give voters real policy choices
Thinking now about politics in (COUNTRY), to what extent do you agree or disagree with the following statements: Referendums are a good way to decide important political questions.
Thinking of the last national election in (COUNTRY), how honest was it regarding the counting and reporting of the votes?
Thinking of the last national election in (COUNTRY), how fair was it regarding the opportunities of the candidates and parties to campaign?
Thinking of the public service in (COUNTRY), how committed is it to serve the people?
How widespread do you think corruption is in the public service in (COUNTRY)?
option_text: A small number of people are involved
A moderate number of people are involved
A lot of people are involved

option_text: Give people more say in government decisions

Q7: Here is a list of some different environmental problems. Which problem, if any, do you think is the most important for [COUNTRY] as a whole?
Q8: There has been a lot of discussion about the world’s climate and the idea it has been changing in recent decades. Which of the following statements comes closest to your opinion?
option_text: The world’s climate has not been changing
Has been changing mostly due to natural processes
Has been changing about equally due to natural processes and human activity
Has been changing mostly due to human activity

question: Q9a: On a scale from 0 to 10, how bad or good do you think the impacts of climate change will be for the world as a whole? (0 means extremely bad, 10 means extremely good)
Q9b: On a scale from 0 to 10, how bad or good do you think the impacts of climate change will be for [COUNTRY]? (0 means extremely bad, 10 means extremely good)
Q10a: How much do you agree or disagree with the following statement: Modern science will solve our environmental problems with little change to our way of life.
Q10b: How much do you agree or disagree with the following statement: We worry too much about the future of the environment and not enough about prices and jobs today.
Q10c: How much do you agree or disagree with the following statement: Almost everything we do in modern life harms the environment.
Q10d: How much do you agree or disagree with the following statement: People worry too much about human progress harming the environment.
Q10e: How much do you agree or disagree with the following statement: In order to protect the environment [COUNTRY] needs economic growth.
Q10f: How much do you agree or disagree with the following statement: Economic growth always harms the environment.
Q11a: How willing would you be to pay much higher prices in order to protect the environment?
Q11b: How willing would you be to pay much higher taxes in order to protect the environment?
Q11c: How willing would you be to accept cuts in your standard of living in order to protect the environment?
Q11d: How willing would you be to accept a reduction in the size of [COUNTRY’s] protected nature areas, in order to open them up for economic development?
Q12a: How much do you agree or disagree with the following statement: It is just too difficult for someone like me to do much about the environment.
Q12b: How much do you agree or disagree with the following statement: I do what is right for the environment, even when it costs more money or takes more time.
Q12c: How much do you agree or disagree with the following statement: There are more important things to do in life than protect the environment.
Q12d: How much do you agree or disagree with the following statement: There is no point in doing what I can for the environment unless others do the same.
Q12e: How much do you agree or disagree with the following statement: Many of the claims about environmental threats are exaggerated.
Q12f: How much do you agree or disagree with the following statement: I find it hard to know whether the way I live is helpful or harmful to the environment.
Q12g: How much do you agree or disagree with the following statement: Environmental problems have a direct effect on my everyday life.

Q13b: In general, do you think that air pollution caused by industry is extremely dangerous for the environment?
Q13c: In general, do you think that pesticides and chemicals used in farming are extremely dangerous for the environment?

Q13f: In general, do you think that modifying the genes of certain crops is extremely dangerous for the environment?
Q13g: In general, do you think that nuclear power stations are extremely dangerous for the environment?
Q14a: Which of these approaches do you think would be the best way of getting business and industry in [COUNTRY] to protect the environment?
option_text: Heavy fines for businesses that damage the environment
Use the tax system to reward businesses that protect the environment
More information and education for businesses about the advantages of protecting the environment

question: Q14b: Which of these approaches do you think would be the best way of getting people and their families in [COUNTRY] to protect the environment?
option_text: Heavy fines for people who damage the environment
Use the tax system to reward people who protect the environment
More information and education for people about the advantages of protecting the environment

question: Q15: How much, if at all, do you enjoy being outside in nature?

R1a: To begin, we have some questions about women. A working mother can establish just as warm and secure a relationship with her children as a mother who does not work.
R1b: To begin, we have some questions about women. A pre-school child is likely to suffer if his or her mother works.
R1c: To begin, we have some questions about women. All in all, family life suffers when the woman has a full-time job.
R1d: To begin, we have some questions about women. A job is all right, but what most women really want is a home and children.
R1e: To begin, we have some questions about women. Being a housewife is just as fulfilling as working for pay.
R2a: And to what extent do you agree or disagree: Both the man and woman should contribute to the household income.
R2b: And to what extent do you agree or disagree: A man's job is to earn money; a woman's job is to look after the home and family.
R3a: Do you think that women should work outside the home full-time, part-time or not at all under the following circumstance: When there is a child under school age.
R3b: Do you think that women should work outside the home full-time, part-time or not at all under the following circumstance: After the youngest child starts school.
R4a: To what extent do you agree or disagree: Married people are generally happier than unmarried people.
R4b: To what extent do you agree or disagree: People who want children ought to get married.
R4c: To what extent do you agree or disagree: It is all right for a couple to live together without intending to get married.
R4d: To what extent do you agree or disagree: Divorce is usually the best solution when a couple can’t seem to work out their marriage problems.
N5a: Children grow up in different kinds of families. To what extent do you agree or disagree: One parent can bring up a child as well as two parents together.
N5b: Children grow up in different kinds of families. To what extent do you agree or disagree: A same sex female couple can bring up a child as well as a male-female couple.
N5c: Children grow up in different kinds of families. To what extent do you agree or disagree: A same sex male couple can bring up a child as well as a male-female couple.
N7a: To what extent do you agree or disagree: Watching children grow up is life's greatest joy.
N7b: To what extent do you agree or disagree: Having children interferes too much with the freedom of parents.
N7c: To what extent do you agree or disagree: Children are a financial burden on their parents.
N7d: To what extent do you agree or disagree: Having children restricts the employment and career chances of one or both parents.
N7e: To what extent do you agree or disagree: Having children increases people’s social standing in society.
N7f: To what extent do you agree or disagree: Adult children are an important source of help for elderly parents.
N9: And who should pay for this leave?
option_text: Both the government and the employer

question: N10: Still thinking about the same couple, if both are in a similar work situation and are eligible for paid leave, how should this paid leave period be divided between the mother and the father?
option_text: The mother should take the entire paid leave period and the father should not take any paid leave
The mother should take most of the paid leave period and the father should take some of it
The mother and the father should each take half of the paid leave period
The father should take most of the paid leave period and the mother should take some of it
The father should take the entire paid leave period and the mother should not take any paid leave

question: N11a: Consider a family with a child under school age. What, in your opinion, is the best way for them to organise their family and work life?
option_text: The mother stays at home and the father works full-time
The mother works part-time and the father works full-time
Both the mother and the father work full-time
Both the mother and the father work part-time
The father works part-time and the mother works full-time
The father stays at home and the mother works full-time

question: N11b: And, in your opinion, which of these options would be the least desirable?
option_text: The mother stays at home and the father works full-time
The mother works part-time and the father works full-time
The father works part-time and the mother works full-time
The father stays at home and the mother works full-time

question: N12: People have different views on childcare for children under school age. Who do you think should primarily provide childcare?
N13: Who do you think should primarily cover the costs of childcare for children under school age?
N14: Thinking about elderly people who need help in their everyday lives (e.g. grocery shopping, cleaning, laundry), who do you think should primarily provide this help?
N15: And who do you think should primarily cover the costs of this help to these elderly people?
option_text: The elderly people themselves or their family

question: R18: How do you and your spouse/partner organise the income that one or both of you receive?
option_text: I manage all the money and give my spouse/partner his/her share
My spouse/partner manages all the money and gives me my share
We pool all the money and each take out what we need
We pool some of the money and keep the rest separate
We each keep our own money separate

question: R19a: In your household, who does the following: Does the laundry?
option_text: Is done by a third person

question: R19b: In your household, who does the following: Makes small repairs around the house?
option_text: Is done by a third person

question: R19c: In your household, who does the following: Cares for sick family members?
option_text: Is done by a third person

question: R19d: In your household, who does the following: Shops for groceries?
option_text: Is done by a third person

question: R19e: In your household, who does the following: Does the household cleaning?
option_text: Is done by a third person

question: R19f: In your household, who does the following: Prepares the meals?
option_text: Is done by a third person

question: R20: Which of the following best applies to the sharing of household work between you and your spouse/partner?
option_text: I do much more than my fair share
I do a bit more than my fair share
I do roughly my fair share
I do a bit less than my fair share
I do much less than my fair share

question: R21: When you and your spouse/partner make decisions about choosing shared weekend activities, who has the final say?
R22: Considering all sources of income, between you and your spouse/partner, who has the higher income?
option_text: I have a much higher income
We have about the same income
My spouse/partner has a higher income
My spouse/partner has a much higher income

question: R23a: How often in the past three months have you come home from work too tired to do the chores which need to be done?

R23c: How often in the past three months have you arrived at work too tired to function well because of the household work you had done?
R23d: How often in the past three months have you found it difficult to concentrate at work because of your family responsibilities?

R25: All things considered, how satisfied are you with your (main) job?
R26: All things considered, how satisfied are you with your family life?
R27: In general, would you say your health is…
R28: Did your mother ever work for pay for as long as one year, after you were born and before you were 14?
R29a: Did you work outside the home when a child was under school age?
R29b: Did you work outside the home after the youngest child started school?
R30a: What about your spouse/partner at that time – did he/she work outside the home when a child was under school age?
R30b: What about your spouse/partner at that time – did he/she work outside the home after the youngest child started school?
R31: Who usually makes/made the decisions about how to bring up your children?
1. If you were to consider your life in general these days, how happy or unhappy would you say you are, on the whole?
2. In general, how much confidence do you have in the health care system in [country]?
3. Is it fair or unfair that people with higher incomes can afford better health care than people with lower incomes?
4a. How much do you agree or disagree with the following statement: People use health care services more than necessary.
4b. How much do you agree or disagree with the following statement: The government should provide only limited health care services.
4c. How much do you agree or disagree with the following statement: In general, the health care system in [country] is inefficient.
5. How willing would you be to pay higher taxes to improve the level of health care for all people in [country]?
6a. How much do you agree or disagree with the following statement: People should have access to publicly funded health care even if they do not hold citizenship of [country].
6b. How much do you agree or disagree with the following statement: People should have access to publicly funded health care even if they behave in ways that damage their health.
7a. In [country], do you think it is easier or harder to get access to health care for rich people than for poor people?
7b. In [country], do you think it is easier or harder to get access to health care for old people than for young people?
7c. In [country], do you think it is easier or harder to get access to health care for women than for men?
7d. In [country], do you think it is easier or harder to get access to health care for citizens of [country] than for people who do not hold [country's] citizenship?

8a. Severe health problems may have many causes. To what extent do you agree or disagree with the following statement: People suffer from severe health problems because they behaved in ways that damaged their health.

8b. Severe health problems may have many causes. To what extent do you agree or disagree with the following statement: People suffer from severe health problems because of the environment they are exposed to at work or where they live.
8c. Severe health problems may have many causes. To what extent do you agree or disagree with the following statement: People suffer from severe health problems because of their genes.
8d. Severe health problems may have many causes. To what extent do you agree or disagree with the following statement: People suffer from severe health problems because they are poor.

9. To what extent do you agree or disagree with the following statement: [Alternative/traditional/folk] medicine provides better solutions for health problems than [mainstream/Western conventional] medicine?
10a. How much do you agree or disagree with the following statement about doctors in general in [country]: All things considered, doctors can be trusted.
10b. How much do you agree or disagree with the following statement about doctors in general in [country]: The medical skills of doctors are not as good as they should be.
10c. How much do you agree or disagree with the following statement about doctors in general in [country]: Doctors care more about their earnings than about their patients.

question: 12a. During the past 12 months, how often, if at all, have you used the internet to look for information on healthy lifestyle?

option_text: Once a month or less often
